pages.raw_text:
The first case tried under the
employers liability act in this state
was decided in a sealed verdict
brought in this morning by the
federal court jury in the case of
Charles N. Roush against the
Great Northern In favor of the
plaintiff. Roush sued for $4,000
damages and got a verdict for $6,-
600. 1t was alleged by Roush that
while coupling cars at Deer Park
the train backed and crushed him.
Carelessness alleged to have been
caused by the drunkenness of the
engineer was alleged.


The employers liability act was
passed by the last session of con-
gress and became a law in June.
1t applies only to interstate COM
merce cases. 1t does away with
the negligence of fellow servants
and modifies the doctrine of con-
tributory negligence. Nearly every
case before the federal court here
before has been thrown out on ac-
count of contributory negligence on
the part of the plaintiffs. The rail-
way attorneys have not yet served
notice of any further action and it
is believed the case will not be
fought any further.


A similar case Steven S. Shields
VS.. Great Northern, was settled out
of court today.
